aliases: War to Resist US Aggression and Aid Korea
description: war between North and South Korea, 1950–1953
description_wiki: The Korean War (25 June 1950 – 27 July 1953) was an armed conflict the Korean Peninsula fought between North Korea (Democratic People's Republic of Korea; DPRK) and South Korea (Republic of Korea; ROK) and their allies.

description: city in Seoul Capital Area, South Korea
description_wiki: Incheon is a city located in northwestern South Korea, bordering Seoul and Gyeonggi Province to the east.

description: formation of the United States Army (since 1917)
description_wiki: The 3rd Infantry Division (3ID) (nicknamed Rock of the Marne) is a combined arms division of the United States Army based at Fort Stewart, Georgia.
The 3d Medical Battalion (3D MED BN) is a medical support unit of the United States Marine Corps and is headquartered at Camp Foster, Okinawa, Japan.
M*A*S*H  is an American war comedy drama television series that aired on CBS from September 17, 1972, to February 28, 1983.

surface_forms: that new book out now, I think, by Halberstam on the Korean War. The Coldest Year
description_wiki: The Coldest Winter: America and the Korean War is a non-fiction book by the author David Halberstam.
Japan is an island country in East Asia.

surface_forms: South Korean soldiers - they were called "ROCs"
aliases: Armée de la République de Corée
description: land warfare branch of South Korea's military
description_wiki: The Republic of Korea Army (ROKA; Korean: 대한민국 육군; Hanja: 大韓民國陸軍; RR: Daehanminguk Yukgun), also known as the ROK Army or South Korean Army, is the army of South Korea, responsible for ground-based warfare.
The Imjin River (Korean: 임진강; South Korean spelling) or Rimjin (림진강; North Korean spelling) is the seventh-largest river in Korea.
Kowang is a village in Mustang District and former village development committee (VDC) in the Dhawalagiri Zone of northern Nepal.
